proportions.depth_chosen_for: Daylight to reach full interior through glass walls

glass_curtain_walls.description: World's first industrially prefabricated curtain wall system
glass_curtain_walls.construction: Steel-and-glass sandwich panels with 3cm Torfoleum insulation
glass_curtain_walls.note: Columns set back from perimeter so glass runs uninterrupted

mushroom_columns.description: Reinforced concrete columns with flared (fungiform) capitals supporting flat slabs
mushroom_columns.advantage: No beams needed, completely open floor plates, saves ~1.5m height per building

conveyor_bridges.description: Glazed aerial walkways connecting production buildings to storage/dispatch hall

production_flow.description: Raw materials enter top floor, each processing stage moves down one floor

curved_office_building.location: Front of complex, connected to factory

block_palette.columns: Light grey concrete, set 1 block inward from glass
block_palette.floor_slabs: Smooth stone or light grey concrete
block_palette.conveyor_bridges: Glass panes with iron bar frame, 2-3 blocks wide

minecraft_suggestions.build_notes: The stepped silhouette (8/5/3 floors descending) is the most recognizable feature. Build three connected rectangular glass boxes of decreasing height.
